<!-- Code adapted from Daniel Padfield https://padpadpadpad.github.io/BayesTraitsAcrAllNodes/ -->

<!-- PhyloXML Arabic tree adapted from Glottolog https://glottolog.org/resource/languoid/id/arab1395 -->

## 1. Setup

# Set working directory
setwd("~/masters/tfm/phylogenetic-analysis-of-coptic-influence-on-egyptian-arabic/scripts")

# Install and load packages
install.packages("here")
install.packages("posterior")
install.packages("tidybayes")
install.packages("coda")
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("rgriff23/btw")
install.packages("tidytree")
install.packages("ape")
install.packages("tidyverse")
install.packages("patchwork")
install.packages("janitor")
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install("treeio")
BiocManager::install("ggtree")

library(here)
library(posterior)
library(tidybayes)
library(coda)
library(btw) # remotes::install_github('rgriff23/btw')
library(tidytree)
library(ape)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(janitor)
library(treeio)
library(ggtree)

# Set where I am in the project
here::i_am('scripts/BayesTraits.Rmd')


## 2. Phylogenetic tree loading

# Load in Arabic tree from PhyloXML file
xml = read.phyloxml(here("data/arab1395.phylo.xml"))
tree = xml@phylo

# Add branch lengths
# Root has 0.0 branch length
branch_lengths <- rep(0.2, 54)
tree$edge.length <- branch_lengths

# Plot tree
tree_plot <- plot(tree, show.node.label = TRUE, root.edge = TRUE)


## 3. Trait data loading

# Read in trait data
trait <- read.table(here('data/trait.txt'), col.names=c("language", "value"))

# Check that the trait table Language column order is the same as the tree tip order
sum(trait$language == tree$tip.label) == nrow(trait)


## 4. BayesTraits input formatting

# Convert tree to a dataframe
tree_data <- as_tibble(tree)

# For each internal node, we want to get the labels of its offspring nodes
# Internal nodes themselves do not have labels in this tree

# Create a dataframe of internal nodes with a new empty column for offspring tip labels
d_nodes <- tibble(nodes = dplyr::filter(tree_data, is.na(label)) %>% pull(node), label = NA)

# Calculate offspring tip labels for each node and fill in label column
for (i in 1:nrow(d_nodes)) {
  temp_node = d_nodes$nodes[i]
  temp_offspring <- offspring(tree_data, temp_node, tiponly = TRUE)
  d_nodes$label[i] <- temp_offspring$label %>% paste0(collapse = ' ')
  }

# Add in the correct commands needed to reconstruct the ancestral states 
d_nodes <- mutate(
  d_nodes, 
  first_command = paste('AddTag', paste('T', nodes, sep = ''), label, sep = ' '), 
  second_command = paste('AddNode', nodes, paste('T', nodes, sep = ''), sep = ' ')
  )

# Output this as a vector
to_save <- select(d_nodes, first_command, second_command) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), values_to = 'command') %>%
  pull(command)

# Save to file that will be input to BayesTraits
write.table(to_save, here('data/trait_tag_nodes.txt'), quote=FALSE, col.names = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)

# Save tree as nexus file as required by BayesTraits
write.nexus(tree, file = here('data/tree.nex'))


## 4. Run maximum likelihood analysis in BayesTraits

# Create ML control file manually and save it as scripts/max_likelihood_control.txt

# In terminal, run:
# ../BayesTraitsV4.0.0-OSX/BayesTraitsV4 ../data/tree.nex ../data/trait.txt < max_likelihood_control.txt


## 5. BayesTraits output reading

# Read in output from BayesTraits
output <- parse_log(here('data/ML.Log.txt'))

# Grab just the nodes and transform into a table with the probability of
# each state for each node
d_output <- select(output$results, starts_with('X')) %>% 
   pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = 'var', values_to = 'prob') %>% 
   filter(!is.na(prob)) %>% 
   separate(col = var, c('node', 'p', 'state'), sep = '\\.') %>% 
   select(-p) %>% 
   mutate(node = gsub('X', '', node))

# Transform into table with two columns for each state probability per node
d_wide <- d_output %>%
  mutate(node = as.numeric(node)) %>%
  pivot_wider(
  names_from = state,
  values_from = prob,
  names_prefix = "prob_"
  )

# Plot tree with trait values and state probabilities
ggtree(tree) %<+% d_wide %<+% rename(trait, label = language) +
  geom_tiplab(size = 3.5) +
  geom_tippoint(aes(fill = factor(value)), shape = 21, size = 2.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "white", "1" = "black"), name = "Trait") +
  geom_nodepoint(aes(color = prob_1)) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  labs(color = "P(1)") +
  geom_nodelab(aes(label = paste0("P(0)=", round(prob_0, 2),
                                  "\n",
                                  "P(1)=", round(prob_1, 2))),
              size = 3.5)
